message:
🐛 Fix llm-d stack discovery and cluster filtering bugs (#597)

* 🌱 Improve fullscreen support for AI-ML graphical cards

- Add AI-ML cards to LARGE_EXPANDED_CARDS for bigger modal
- Add overflow:visible to SVG elements (LLMdFlow, EPPRouting)
- Expand viewBox dimensions to reduce clipping
- Add flex-1 to card containers for better sizing

Note: Further investigation needed for complete fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🌱 Mark LLMdConfigurator as demo-only card

The configurator shows tuning option presets but generates simplified
YAML, not complete llm-d deployment configs. Mark as demo until it
can generate real InferencePool/Gateway/EPP configurations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ✨ Add rich context to AI mission suggestion prompts

- Security issues: Changed from navigate to AI action, includes issue
  details, cluster info, and specific remediation request
- Restarting pods: Added namespace, restart count, status, and
  troubleshooting guidance (logs, probes, OOM)
- Unavailable deployments: Added namespace, replica counts, and
  diagnostic guidance (events, images, resources)
- Unhealthy clusters: Added reachability status, error messages, and
  troubleshooting areas (API, control plane, certs)
- Resource limits: Added sample pods and explanation of risks
- Node pressure: Added specific condition types and remediation guidance
- WebSocket payload: Now includes mission context for agent use

This ensures AI missions receive sufficient context for efficient
token usage and better diagnosis quality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ✨ Add snooze functionality to Hardware Health alerts

Features:
- Per-alert snooze with selectable duration (5m, 15m, 1h, 4h, 24h)
- "Snooze All" option for batch snoozing visible alerts
- Snoozed count indicator with toggle to show/hide snoozed
- Snooze time remaining display on snoozed alerts
- Click snoozed indicator to unsnooze
- "Clear all snoozes" option
- Persisted to localStorage, auto-expires when time elapses

New hook: useSnoozedAlerts with module-level state sharing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ✨ Add Mac notification deep linking to KS console

- New useDeepLink hook to handle URL-based deep linking
- Notification click now opens node drilldown with context
- URL params: ?drilldown=node&cluster=xyz&node=abc&issue=...
- Layout component processes deep link params on mount
- Supports node, pod, cluster, deployment, namespace drilldowns

When clicking Mac notifications, the console opens directly
to the relevant drilldown view with issue context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Add demo data indicator and refresh improvements to GitHub CI Monitor

- Show yellow banner when no GitHub token configured with link to settings
- Add "Updated X ago" timestamp when using live data
- Improve refresh button visual feedback
- Expose refresh method via forwardRef
- Use red styling for errors vs yellow for demo warning

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ✨ Add deep link focus for GitHub token settings

- Add Token button now navigates to /settings#github-token
- GitHubTokenSection detects hash and auto-scrolls with focus
- Adds purple ring highlight effect when focused via deep link
- Cleans up URL after navigation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix GitHub token section centering in viewport

- Use manual scroll calculation instead of scrollIntoView
- Find the main scrollable container and calculate center offset
- Properly centers section even when near bottom of page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Improve GitHub token section scroll centering

- Use main element's clientHeight for viewport calculation
- Calculate center position accounting for element height
- Increase timeout for page to fully load before scrolling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix GitHub token scroll by targeting next section

- Add id="system-updates-settings" to UpdateSettings component
- Scroll to next section so GitHub token appears at top of viewport
- Simpler and more reliable than trying to calculate center position

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🎨 Center GitHub token section by scrolling next section to center

- Change block: 'start' to block: 'center' for System Updates
- This positions GitHub Integration more centered in viewport

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix GitHub token decoding and add auto-refresh on token change

- Token is stored base64 encoded, need to decode before using
- Add storage event listener to auto-refresh when token changes
- Card now updates automatically when user adds token in Settings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ✨ Add full rotation animation to refresh button

- Use rotation counter state to drive CSS transform
- Each click rotates icon 360 degrees with smooth transition
- Purple highlight while refreshing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🎨 Match refresh animation to unified control (1s duration)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🗑️ Remove redundant refresh button from GitHub CI Monitor

CardWrapper header already provides unified refresh control

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ✨ Add inline repo configuration for GitHub CI Monitor

- Click "X repos" to toggle inline editor
- Add new repos with owner/repo format validation
- Remove repos (keeps at least one)
- Persist to localStorage
- Auto-refresh when repos change

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ✨ Add AI diagnose button for failed GitHub workflows

- Stethoscope icon appears on failed/timed_out workflows
- Opens AI Mission with context about the failure
- Clear workflow data immediately when repo is removed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ✨ Add diagnose buttons to issue items, remove generic section

- Add stethoscope icon to each issue in WorkloadMonitorAlerts
- Remove generic AI Diagnose section from bottom of card
- Pass monitorType to alerts for context in AI prompts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix GitHub Activity card: token decode, merged PRs, inline CRUD, refresh

- Add base64 token decoding (Settings stores tokens encoded)
- Fix merged PRs count by using merged_at field (not merged boolean)
- Fetch open and closed PRs separately to ensure merged PRs included
- Replace dropdown/modal repo UI with inline CRUD (matches CI Monitor)
- Add forwardRef + useImperativeHandle for card refresh button
- Add 60-second auto-refresh interval for fresh data
- Reduce cache TTL from 30 to 5 minutes
- Add cache version (v2) to invalidate stale entries
- Simplify issue count to use fetched data directly

Also adds helper functions for cluster token expired detection:
- isClusterTokenExpired() - auth error type check
- isClusterNetworkOffline() - network vs auth differentiation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ✨ Sort open PRs and issues first in GitHub Activity card

Open items now appear at the top of their respective lists,
making it easier to see actionable items without pagination.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix llm-d stack discovery and cluster filtering bugs

- Remove hardcoded namespace list in useCachedData.ts and useLLMd.ts
  - Now fetches deployments from all namespaces with -A flag
  - Expanded namespace pattern detection for llm-d workloads
  - Added llm-d.ai/role label detection

- Fix cluster filter not filtering issues in LLMdStackMonitor
  - Issues now explicitly filtered by selected clusters
  - Changed dependency from filteredServers to servers for proper reactivity

- Fix stack discovery caching to preserve data when cluster unreachable
  - Added comment clarifying preservation of P/D/WVA details

- Optimize StackSelector dropdown performance
  - Memoized StackOption component
  - Added useCallback for selection handler

- Fix localStorage QuotaExceededError in useMetricsHistory
  - Added try/catch with graceful degradation strategies

- Fix agent selector greyed out incorrectly
  - Only grey out in demo mode, not during loading

- Capitalize PROW in all card titles

- Improve GitHub Activity card
  - Visual distinction for open vs closed issues
  - Open issues sorted to top

- Add full screen support for AI-ML cards
  - Cards detect expanded state and increase min-height

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,209 @@
+import { useEffect, useCallback } from 'react'
+import { useSearchParams, useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'
+import { useDrillDownActions } from './useDrillDown'
+
+/**
+ * Deep linking support for notifications and external links.
+ *
+ * URL params supported:
+ * - ?drilldown=node&cluster=xyz&node=abc - Opens node drilldown
+ * - ?drilldown=pod&cluster=xyz&namespace=abc&pod=def - Opens pod drilldown
+ * - ?drilldown=cluster&cluster=xyz - Opens cluster drilldown
+ * - ?drilldown=deployment&cluster=xyz&namespace=abc&deployment=def - Opens deployment drilldown
+ * - ?action=offline-detection - Navigates to dashboard with offline detection focus
+ * - ?action=hardware-health - Navigates to dashboard with hardware health focus
+ */
+
+export type DeepLinkAction =
+  | 'offline-detection'
+  | 'hardware-health'
+  | 'security'
+  | 'alerts'
+
+export type DeepLinkDrilldown =
+  | 'node'
+  | 'pod'
+  | 'cluster'
+  | 'deployment'
+  | 'namespace'
+
+interface DeepLinkParams {
+  drilldown?: DeepLinkDrilldown
+  action?: DeepLinkAction
+  cluster?: string
+  namespace?: string
+  node?: string
+  pod?: string
+  deployment?: string
+  // Additional context
+  issue?: string
+}
+
+/**
+ * Build a deep link URL for notifications
+ */
+export function buildDeepLinkURL(params: DeepLinkParams): string {
+  const base = window.location.origin + window.location.pathname
+  const searchParams = new URLSearchParams()
+
+  if (params.drilldown) searchParams.set('drilldown', params.drilldown)
+  if (params.action) searchParams.set('action', params.action)
+  if (params.cluster) searchParams.set('cluster', params.cluster)
+  if (params.namespace) searchParams.set('namespace', params.namespace)
+  if (params.node) searchParams.set('node', params.node)
+  if (params.pod) searchParams.set('pod', params.pod)
+  if (params.deployment) searchParams.set('deployment', params.deployment)
+  if (params.issue) searchParams.set('issue', params.issue)
+
+  return `${base}?${searchParams.toString()}`
+}
+
+/**
+ * Send a browser notification with deep link support
+ */
+export function sendNotificationWithDeepLink(
+  title: string,
+  body: string,
+  params: DeepLinkParams,
+  options?: NotificationOptions
+): Notification | null {
+  if (!('Notification' in window) || Notification.permission !== 'granted') {
+    return null
+  }
+
+  const notification = new Notification(title, {
+    body,
+    icon: '/kubestellar-logo.svg',
+    requireInteraction: true,
+    ...options,
+  })
+
+  notification.onclick = () => {
+    // Focus existing window or open new one
+    window.focus()
+
+    // Navigate to deep link
+    const url = buildDeepLinkURL(params)
+    window.location.href = url
+
+    notification.close()
+  }
+
+  return notification
+}
+
+/**
+ * Hook to handle incoming deep links
+ */
+export function useDeepLink() {
+  const [searchParams, setSearchParams] = useSearchParams()
+  const navigate = useNavigate()
+  const { drillToNode, drillToPod, drillToCluster, drillToDeployment, drillToNamespace } = useDrillDownActions()
+
+  // Process deep link params on mount
+  useEffect(() => {
+    const drilldown = searchParams.get('drilldown') as DeepLinkDrilldown | null
+    const action = searchParams.get('action') as DeepLinkAction | null
+    const cluster = searchParams.get('cluster')
+    const namespace = searchParams.get('namespace')
+    const node = searchParams.get('node')
+    const pod = searchParams.get('pod')
+    const deployment = searchParams.get('deployment')
+    const issue = searchParams.get('issue')
+
+    // Clear params after processing to avoid re-triggering
+    const clearParams = () => {
+      const newParams = new URLSearchParams(searchParams)
+      newParams.delete('drilldown')
+      newParams.delete('action')
+      newParams.delete('cluster')
+      newParams.delete('namespace')
+      newParams.delete('node')
+      newParams.delete('pod')
+      newParams.delete('deployment')
+      newParams.delete('issue')
+      setSearchParams(newParams, { replace: true })
+    }
+
+    // Handle action-based navigation
+    if (action) {
+      switch (action) {
+        case 'offline-detection':
+          // Navigate to dashboard - the card will be visible
+          navigate('/')
+          break
+        case 'hardware-health':
+          navigate('/')
+          break
+        case 'security':
+          navigate('/security')
+          break
+        case 'alerts':
+          navigate('/alerts')
+          break
+      }
+      clearParams()
+      return
+    }
+
+    // Handle drilldown navigation
+    if (drilldown && cluster) {
+      // Small delay to ensure the app is ready
+      const timer = setTimeout(() => {
+        const issueData = issue ? { issue } : undefined
+
+        switch (drilldown) {
+          case 'node':
+            if (node) {
+              drillToNode(cluster, node, issueData)
+            }
+            break
+          case 'pod':
+            if (namespace && pod) {
+              drillToPod(cluster, namespace, pod, issueData)
+            }
+            break
+          case 'cluster':
+            drillToCluster(cluster, issueData)
+            break
+          case 'deployment':
+            if (namespace && deployment) {
+              drillToDeployment(cluster, namespace, deployment, issueData)
+            }
+            break
+          case 'namespace':
+            if (namespace) {
+              drillToNamespace(cluster, namespace)
+            }
+            break
+        }
+
+        clearParams()
+      }, 500) // Wait for app to initialize
+
+      return () => clearTimeout(timer)
+    }
+  }, [searchParams, setSearchParams, navigate, drillToNode, drillToPod, drillToCluster, drillToDeployment, drillToNamespace])
+
+  // Build deep link URL helper
+  const buildURL = useCallback((params: DeepLinkParams) => {
+    return buildDeepLinkURL(params)
+  }, [])
+
+  // Send notification helper
+  const sendNotification = useCallback((
+    title: string,
+    body: string,
+    params: DeepLinkParams,
+    options?: NotificationOptions
+  ) => {
+    return sendNotificationWithDeepLink(title, body, params, options)
+  }, [])
+
+  return {
+    buildURL,
+    sendNotification,
+  }
+}
+
+export default useDeepLink

@@ -225,31 +225,23 @@ export function useLLMdServers(clusters: string[] = ['vllm-d', 'platform-eval'])
         try {
           console.log(`[useLLMdServers] Fetching from ${cluster}`)
 
-          // Fetch deployments from key llm-d namespaces only
-          const keyNamespaces = [
-            'b2', 'e2e-helm', 'e2e-solution', 'e2e-pd', 'effi', 'effi2', 'guygir',
-            'llm-d', 'aibrix-system', 'hc4ai-operator', 'hc4ai-operator-dev',
-            'e2e-solution-platform-eval', 'inference-router-test'
-          ]

           const allDeployments: DeploymentResource[] = []
 
-          // Fetch sequentially to avoid queue issues

-            try {
-              console.log(`[useLLMdServers] Fetching deployments from ${cluster}/${ns}`)
-              const resp = await kubectlProxy.exec(
-                ['get', 'deployments', '-n', ns, '-o', 'json'],
-                { context: cluster, timeout: 10000 }
-              )
-              if (resp.exitCode === 0 && resp.output) {
-                const data = JSON.parse(resp.output)
-                const items = data.items || []

@@ -0,0 +1,178 @@
+import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react'
+
+const STORAGE_KEY = 'kubestellar-snoozed-alerts'
+
+// Snooze duration options in milliseconds
+export const SNOOZE_DURATIONS = {
+  '5m': 5 * 60 * 1000,
+  '15m': 15 * 60 * 1000,
+  '1h': 60 * 60 * 1000,
+  '4h': 4 * 60 * 60 * 1000,
+  '24h': 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000,
+} as const
+
+export type SnoozeDuration = keyof typeof SNOOZE_DURATIONS
+
+export interface SnoozedAlert {
+  alertId: string
+  snoozedAt: number // timestamp
+  expiresAt: number // timestamp
+  duration: SnoozeDuration
+}
+
+interface StoredState {
+  snoozed: SnoozedAlert[]
+}
+
+// Module-level state for cross-component sharing
+let state: StoredState = { snoozed: [] }
+const listeners: Set<() => void> = new Set()
+
+function notifyListeners() {
+  listeners.forEach((listener) => listener())
+}
+
+function loadState(): StoredState {
+  try {
+    const stored = localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY)
+    if (stored) {
+      const parsed = JSON.parse(stored)
+      // Clean up expired snoozes
+      const now = Date.now()
+      parsed.snoozed = (parsed.snoozed || []).filter(
+        (s: SnoozedAlert) => s.expiresAt > now
+      )
+      return parsed
+    }
+  } catch {
+    // Ignore parse errors
+  }
+  return { snoozed: [] }
+}
+
+function saveState() {
+  localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(state))
+}
+
+// Initialize on module load
+state = loadState()
+
+export function useSnoozedAlerts() {
+  const [localState, setLocalState] = useState<StoredState>(state)
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    const listener = () => setLocalState({ ...state })
+    listeners.add(listener)
+
+    // Set up timer to auto-refresh when snoozes expire
+    const checkExpired = () => {
+      const now = Date.now()
+      const hadExpired = state.snoozed.some(s => s.expiresAt <= now)
+      if (hadExpired) {
+        state.snoozed = state.snoozed.filter(s => s.expiresAt > now)
+        saveState()
+        notifyListeners()
+      }
+    }
+
+    // Check every minute
+    const intervalId = setInterval(checkExpired, 60000)
+
+    return () => {
+      listeners.delete(listener)
+      clearInterval(intervalId)
+    }
+  }, [])
+
+  const snoozeAlert = useCallback((alertId: string, duration: SnoozeDuration = '1h') => {
+    // Remove existing snooze if present
+    state.snoozed = state.snoozed.filter(s => s.alertId !== alertId)
+
+    const now = Date.now()
+    const newSnoozed: SnoozedAlert = {
+      alertId,
+      snoozedAt: now,
+      expiresAt: now + SNOOZE_DURATIONS[duration],
+      duration,
+    }
+    state.snoozed = [...state.snoozed, newSnoozed]
+    saveState()
+    notifyListeners()
+    return newSnoozed
+  }, [])
+
+  const snoozeMultiple = useCallback((alertIds: string[], duration: SnoozeDuration = '1h') => {
+    const now = Date.now()
+    const expiresAt = now + SNOOZE_DURATIONS[duration]
+
+    // Remove existing snoozes for these alerts
+    state.snoozed = state.snoozed.filter(s => !alertIds.includes(s.alertId))
+
+    // Add new snoozes
+    const newSnoozed: SnoozedAlert[] = alertIds.map(alertId => ({
+      alertId,
+      snoozedAt: now,
+      expiresAt,
+      duration,
+    }))
+
+    state.snoozed = [...state.snoozed, ...newSnoozed]
+    saveState()
+    notifyListeners()
+  }, [])
+
+  const unsnoozeAlert = useCallback((alertId: string) => {
+    state.snoozed = state.snoozed.filter(s => s.alertId !== alertId)
+    saveState()
+    notifyListeners()
+  }, [])
+
+  const isSnoozed = useCallback((alertId: string): boolean => {
+    const now = Date.now()
+    return state.snoozed.some(s => s.alertId === alertId && s.expiresAt > now)
+  }, [])
+
+  const getSnoozedAlert = useCallback((alertId: string): SnoozedAlert | null => {
+    const now = Date.now()
+    return state.snoozed.find(s => s.alertId === alertId && s.expiresAt > now) || null
+  }, [])
+
+  const clearAllSnoozed = useCallback(() => {
+    state.snoozed = []
+    saveState()
+    notifyListeners()
+  }, [])
+
+  // Get time remaining on snooze
+  const getSnoozeRemaining = useCallback((alertId: string): number | null => {
+    const snoozed = state.snoozed.find(s => s.alertId === alertId)
+    if (!snoozed) return null
+    return Math.max(0, snoozed.expiresAt - Date.now())
+  }, [])
+
+  return {
+    snoozedAlerts: localState.snoozed,
+    snoozedCount: localState.snoozed.length,
+    snoozeAlert,
+    snoozeMultiple,
+    unsnoozeAlert,
+    isSnoozed,
+    getSnoozedAlert,
+    clearAllSnoozed,
+    getSnoozeRemaining,
+  }
+}
+
+// Helper to format time remaining
+export function formatSnoozeRemaining(ms: number): string {
+  const hours = Math.floor(ms / (60 * 60 * 1000))
+  const minutes = Math.floor((ms % (60 * 60 * 1000)) / (60 * 1000))
+
+  if (hours > 0) {
+    return `${hours}h ${minutes}m`
+  }
+  if (minutes > 0) {
+    return `${minutes}m`
+  }
+  return '<1m'
+}
